story.soThat: tenant ID is available throughout request processing without parameter passing
story.tasks: - AC1: framework/multi-tenancy module created - AC2: TenantId.kt value object with validation - AC3: TenantContext.kt manages ThreadLocal storage with stack-based context - AC4: TenantContextHolder.kt provides static access (get/set/clear methods) - AC5: WeakReference used for memory safety (prevent ThreadLocal leaks) - AC6: Unit tests validate: set context → retrieve → clear - AC7: Thread isolation validated (context not shared between threads) - AC8: Context cleared after request completion (filter cleanup)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. framework/multi-tenancy module created 2. TenantId.kt value object with validation 3. TenantContext.kt manages ThreadLocal storage with stack-based context 4. TenantContextHolder.kt provides static access (get/set/clear methods) 5. WeakReference used for memory safety (prevent ThreadLocal leaks) 6. Unit tests validate: set context → retrieve → clear 7. Thread isolation validated (context not shared between threads) 8. Context cleared after request completion (filter cleanup)

doc.relevance: Defines 3-layer tenant isolation architecture and ThreadLocal management patterns
doc.snippet: Layer 1: TenantContextFilter extracts tenant_id from JWT → ThreadLocal - ThreadLocal-based storage with stack-based context for nested scenarios - WeakReference for memory safety (prevent ThreadLocal leaks) - Fail-closed design: missing tenant context rejects request

doc.section: FR004 - Multi-Tenancy with Isolation and Quotas
doc.relevance: Business requirements for tenant isolation and context management
doc.snippet: Multi-tenant architecture with complete data isolation between tenants. Defense-in-depth approach with 3 layers of tenant validation.
TenantContext API provides fail-closed getCurrentTenantId() and nullable current() Stack-based context allows nested tenant scenarios for testing

doc.relevance: Recent patterns from Epic 3 JWT validation
doc.snippet: Epic 3 completed 10-layer JWT validation with tenant_id claim extraction. JWT validation extracts tenant_id from claims (Layer 4: Claims Schema Validation). All Epic 3 tests use Kotest + Testcontainers with real Keycloak.

Epic 4 implements comprehensive multi-tenancy with defense-in-depth isolation through three layers. Story 4.1: TenantContext and ThreadLocal Management with WeakReference for memory safety. TenantContext.kt manages ThreadLocal storage with stack-based context. Thread isolation validated (context not shared between threads).

artifact.reason: Reference for module structure and Spring configuration patterns from Epic 3
Epic 3 validation patterns for extracting claims from JWT (tenant_id extraction model)
Domain value object patterns for TenantId implementation
Kotest FunSpec patterns from Epic 3 for unit testing

constraints.constraint: Fail-closed design: Missing or invalid tenant context must result in immediate rejection. TenantContext.getCurrentTenantId() throws exception if missing (fail-closed). TenantContext.current() returns null if missing (nullable, for defensive checks).

ThreadLocal must use WeakReference for memory safety to prevent ThreadLocal leaks. Context must be cleared after request completion (handled by filter in Story 4.2). Thread isolation: context must not leak between threads.
Stack-based context allows nested tenant scenarios (useful for testing). Context can be pushed/popped for test scenarios requiring tenant switching.
Zero-tolerance policy: NO wildcard imports. Every import must be explicit (ktlint enforced).
Use Kotest ONLY for all tests. JUnit is explicitly forbidden. Never mix JUnit and Kotest annotations (JUnit @Disabled has no effect on Kotest tests).
TenantId.kt must validate tenant ID format (alphanumeric, reasonable length). Use data class with init block for validation. Throw IllegalArgumentException for invalid tenant IDs.

interface.name: TenantContext API
interface.description: Primary API for tenant context management in multi-tenant applications. Two access patterns: fail-closed (getCurrentTenantId) and nullable (current).

methods.method: fun setCurrentTenantId(tenantId: String) // Stack operation: push tenant context (handled by Layer 1 filter)
fun clearCurrentTenant() // Stack operation: pop tenant context (handled by Layer 1 filter cleanup)

interface.name: TenantId Value Object
interface.description: Validated value object for tenant identifiers. Ensures type safety and validation for tenant IDs.

standards.standard: Constitutional TDD: Test-first development mandatory (Red-Green-Refactor cycle). All production code must be preceded by failing tests. Coverage targets: 85% line coverage, 60-70% mutation coverage.
Use Nullable Design Pattern for fast unit tests. No infrastructure dependencies in unit tests. Integration tests use Testcontainers for real dependencies.
Thread isolation must be validated with concurrent test execution. Multiple threads must not share tenant context. Use Kotlin coroutines or java.util.concurrent for thread testing.

ideas.idea: Test AC6: set context → retrieve → clear cycle
Test AC7: Thread isolation - spawn multiple threads, verify contexts don't leak
Test AC8: Context cleanup - verify ThreadLocal cleared after use
Test nested context scenarios: push context A, push context B, pop B → context A restored
Test TenantId validation: blank ID, invalid characters, too long, valid ID
Test getCurrentTenantId() fail-closed behavior: throws exception when missing
Test current() nullable behavior: returns null when missing
Property-based test for TenantId validation with Kotest Arb generators
